items.title: Woeste Gladbach-middenvelder wil al tijdens de wedstrijd shirtje ruilen
items.summary: Shirtje ruilen doe je… juist: na de wedstrijd. Althans, normale mensen dan. Kevin Stöger, middenvelder van Borussia Mönchengladbach, stond zaterdag tijdens de wedstrijd al met het shirt van tegenstander Exequiel Palacios in zijn handen. Dat was dan ook niet helemaal de bedoeling.

items.title: VI Live: Hubner geeft gelijkmaker cadeau aan Sparta
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-wedstrijden op het programma, maar ook in het buitenland wordt gevoetbald. Onder meer Manchester United Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund en Napoli komen in actie. Volg al het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Nainggolan (37) denkt dat hij nog altijd in Serie A zou kunnen spelen
items.summary: Radja Nainggolan voetbalt tegenwoordig op het tweede niveau van België , maar denkt dat hij nog steeds in de Serie A had kunnen spelen. De 37-jarige routinier vindt dat het niveau van de Serie A enorm achteruit is gegaan.

items.title: Flick hield De Jong uit voorzorg aan de kant: 'Hij was niet helemaal fris'
items.summary: Barcelona boekte zaterdagmiddag een vrij eenvoudige zege op Real Mallorca ( 3-0 ) en dat deden de Catalanen zonder Frenkie de Jong. De Oranje-international was niet helemaal fit en dus hield trainer Hansi Flick hem uit voorzorg aan de kant.

items.title: Celtic require extra time to book Scottish Cup QF spot
items.summary: Celtic needed extra time to advance to the quarter-finals of the Scottish Cup after a dramatic 2-1 come-from-behind victory over Dundee in front of a half-empty Celtic Park as St Mirren, Dunfermline and Partick Thistle also progressed to the last eight.

items.title: Heerenveen gewillig slachtoffer van Twente in zeldzaam doelpuntenspektakel
items.summary: FC Twente heeft zaterdagavond een zeer eenvoudige zege op SC Heerenveen geboekt. In Enschede rekende de thuisclub af met het moeilijk tot scoren komen-syndroom .

items.title: Honest Howe: I'm not doing my job well enough at Newcastle
items.summary: Eddie Howe has admitted he is "not doing my job well enough at the moment" as the pressure on the Newcastle head coach mounted after a 3-2 defeat to Brentford.

items.title: Carrick: Utd haven't 'cracked it' after Spurs win
items.summary: Michael Carrick is not being fooled into thinking Manchester United have "cracked it" after his dream start continued by beating a Tottenham side that captain Cristian Romero apologised to after his costly red card.

items.title: Barça teen Bernal celebrates 'dream' first goal
items.summary: Marc Bernal celebrated a dream moment as the midfielder stepped up his recovery from an ACL injury by scoring his first Barcelona goal in Saturday's 3-0 LaLiga win over Mallorca.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal's 9-point lead means 'nothing'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has claimed Arsenal's nine-point lead at the top of the Premier League means "nothing" despite his team piling the pressure on Manchester City.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: European football: Jacquet sustains ‘serious’ injury days after agreeing Liverpool deal
items.summary: 20-year-old Rennes defender fell awkwardly on shoulder Barcelona outclass Mallorca; Hojlund stars for Napoli Liverpool’s new summer signing Jeremy Jacquet has sustained a “serious” shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye. The 20-year-old fell awkwardly in the second half of a 3-1 defeat to Lens in Ligue 1 and appeared in agony as he left the pitch. “For Jeremy, it’s his shoulder, and for Abdelhamid [Ait Boudlal, the other Rennes player injured] it’s muscular,” Beye said in his post-match interview. “We’ll have time to see, but it’s definitely quite serious for both of them.” Continue reading...

items.title: 'I'm going to be raging when I get beat' - Greaves celebrates milestone win
items.summary: Beau Greaves extended her unprecedented winning run on the PDC women's series to 100 matches with victory in the year's opening Women's Series double-header in Germany.

items.title: Outtara scores late to give Brentford win over Newcastle
items.summary: Dango Outtara scores in the 85th minute to give Brentford a 3-2 win over Newcastle United, as the visitors win their first game at St James' Park since 1934.

items.title: Everton produce second-half comeback to beat Fulham
items.summary: Everton score twice in the final fifteen minutes to secure a 2-1 away win over Fulham at Craven Cottage in the Premier League.

items.title: Gyokeres scores twice as Arsenal beat Sunderland
items.summary: Viktor Gyokeres scores two goals as Arsenal comfortably beat Sunderland 3-0 to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table to nine points.

items.title: Romero red helps Man Utd continue perfect start under Carrick
items.summary: Goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Bruno Fernandes help Manchester United to a 2-0 win over 10-man Tottenham, who saw captain Cristian Romero sent off for the second time this season in the Premier League.

items.title: Palmer scores first-half hat-trick as Chelsea beat Wolves
items.summary: Cole Palmer becomes the first player in Premier League history to score three career first-half hat-tricks, as Chelsea see off Wolves 3-1 at Molineux.

items.title: Liverpool v City is no longer the Premier League’s big show: how have the mighty fallen? | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has led the way with his tactics for a decade but he has changed course and Arsenal have taken advantage Great rivalries are always more about feel than about numbers. There have been only four Premier League seasons in which Manchester City and Liverpool have finished in the top two positions in the table (and one of those occasions was 2013-14 when the managers were Manuel Pellegrini and Brendan Rodgers, which is not a duel anybody is writing books or making documentaries about). Yet for most of the decade that Pep Guardiola has been at City, it has felt that English football was defined by his struggle with Jürgen Klopp and Liverpool, and by a form of the game that developed as each learned from the other. Continue reading...

items.title: Dango Ouattara tips seesaw contest Brentford’s way to sink Newcastle
items.summary: What price Keith Andrews for manager of the season? Thomas Frank’s surprise successor certainly added to his fanclub as he choreographed Brentford’s fourth win in six Premier League games to leave Eddie Howe even gloomier than the unremittingly wet Tyneside weather. While outstanding performances from Dango Ouattara and Keane Lewis-Potter left Andrews’s seventh-placed side appearing genuine European contenders, Howe’s Newcastle have won only one of their last eight matches in all competitions and lost four of their past five. Continue reading...
